Job description:



The Opportunity -Be part of a team inspired by possibility.

We're Looking For
A Strategic Partner Lead with a financial accounting background or finance transformation project experience to lead enterprise strategy, governance, and performance for Stockland's strategic partnership with TCS across core services including Finance and Legal.

What You'll Do
• Evolve the enterprise vision, strategy, and transformation roadmap for Stockland's strategic partnership with TCS across allocated functions.
• Own and evolve the enterprise governance framework, including performance management, reporting, forums, and escalation pathways.
• Act as the primary point of contact for TCS on scope, governance, service delivery, and performance, ensuring alignment to Statements of Work and KPIs.
• Drive continuous improvement, innovation, and transformation initiatives, with a strong focus on outcomes and enterprise value.
• Partner closely with Functional General Managers to understand challenges, manage risks and issues, and enable effective service delivery and change execution.

About You
You're motivated by complex, enterprise scale work and enjoy influencing senior stakeholders to achieve meaningful outcomes. You thrive in environments where strong governance, commercial acumen, and finance insight enable transformation and long term value.

Experience & Skills That Count
• Tertiary qualifications in Finance or Accounting, Business, Commercial Law, or a related discipline.
• Experience in engaging with enterprise level strategic partnerships or complex vendor relationships.
• Finance transformation, finance operations, or financial accounting experience within large or complex organisations.
• Strong governance, performance management, and escalation management capability.
• Commercial acumen with experience managing contracts, Statements of Work, and vendor performance.

The Right Mindset
• Enterprise focused thinker who prioritises whole of business outcomes.
• Confident influencer who builds trusted relationships and navigates complexity.
• Continuous improvement mindset with a passion for innovation and transformation.

Apply Today

Head of Data Insights




Own the enterprise data and capability of an ASX-listed organisation.

This is a permanent role available in QLD or NSW with hybrid and flexible working arrangements.

This is a rare opportunity to step into a newly created senior leadership role with genuine end-to-end ownership. You will define and deliver the data, analytics and responsible AI agenda—shaping how decisions are made, how leaders operate, and how the business grows. Backed by strong executive sponsorship and a clear transformation mandate, this role offers the chance to turn data into a strategic asset with organisation-wide impact.

About the Role

Reporting to the EGM, Digital and Data Transformation, the Head of Data Insights is responsible for defining and delivering the organisation’s data, analytics and AI strategy. The role combines strategic leadership with hands-on delivery and enterprise influence.

Key Responsibilities

Define and execute the enterprise Data & Insights strategy aligned to the 5-year transformation roadmap

Design and deliver a modern enterprise data platform (warehouse or lakehouse)

Establish and embed data governance, ownership, quality, retention and destruction policies

Lead responsible adoption of AI and automation across the organisation

Partner with senior leaders to translate data capability into commercial outcomes

Build, lead and develop a high-performing Data & AI team

About You

You are an experienced data leader with strong executive presence and the ability to influence change in complex environments.

 You will bring:

8+ years’ experience leading data, analytics or information management functions

A proven background delivering enterprise data platform or warehouse transformations

Strong expertise in data governance, privacy, security and regulatory compliance

Solid technical foundations across data architecture, analytics and cloud platforms

Exceptional stakeholder management and communication skills

 Highly regarded experience:

Retail, automotive or customer‑centric industries

Scaling automation across AI/ML, RPA and process redesign

Tools such as Power BI and modern data orchestration platforms

Why Join MotorCycle Holdings?

Greenfield opportunity to shape enterprise data and AI capability

Executive‑level impact with direct access to senior leadership

Hybrid working with flexibility across NSW or QLD

Join a values‑led organisation that prioritises accountability, excellence, integrity and collaboration

Ready to Make an Impact?

If you’re looking for a career‑defining role where you can shape strategy, build capability and see your work influence real business outcomes, we’d love to hear from you.

Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

As a Senior Strategy Manager at SEEK, you'll play a pivotal role in defining and delivering our long-term vision and growth ambitions. Focused on topics within SEEK's APAC product strategy as part of the Strategy Operations Centre of Gravity, you'll tackle some of our most critical and complex challenges, spanning multiple product and AI domains.

You'll lead deep strategic thinking, apply analytical rigour, and turn insights into action. This role suits someone who thrives in ambiguity, is energised by solving complex business problems, and is excited by the opportunity to shape SEEK's future at scale.

What You'll Be Doing

✅ Lead strategy projects spanning multiple product and AI domains, becoming the 'go-to' expert for senior leadership and the broader organisation
✅ Develop and apply analytical frameworks to investigate business issues, identify solutions and make recommendations
✅ Create situational awareness across the organisation through strategic metrics, key results, industry trends and competitor analysis
✅ Communicate strategy with simplicity and impact to all levels—from senior executives to delivery teams
✅ Collaborate in project teams alongside Product, AI and Technology stakeholders to design, test and iterate future solutions
✅ Be a vocal advocate for the strategy, providing guidance to execution teams to ensure alignment

The Opportunity

You'll join SEEK's global Strategy team, a high-performing group of ~40 ex-consultants from premier firms, working across APAC.  With exposure to some of SEEK's most senior leaders, including the Head of Strategy Operations, Directors of Strategy, and Group Executives of Product, AI and Technology, you'll have a rare opportunity to shape the long-term direction of one of the region's most recognised tech brands. Our team culture is caring, curious and impact-oriented, and we're united by a shared purpose: helping people live more fulfilling and productive working lives.

Qualifications

✔ 5-8 years' experience in strategy-related roles, with 3-4 years in a top-tier strategy consulting firm
✔ Highly developed conceptual reasoning, analytical and problem-solving skills with proven ability to frame strategic problems
✔ Experience in developing business-level strategy and breaking this down into objectives, targets and initiatives
✔ Track record of delivering results through cross-functional teams
✔ Ability to influence and build support for fact-based recommendations with diverse stakeholders
✔ High learning orientation and desire to become a global expert in online marketplace design and operations
✔ Passion for growth strategy and excellent business acumen, with experience leading complex and ambiguous strategic projects
✔ Experience in media, internet or tech industries is a plus

Additional Information

At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners
The opportunity to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per financial year
Free income protection insurance

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

Bayside Health is a public health service delivering high-quality care across every stage of life for close to 1.2 million people living in metropolitan Melbourne, the Mornington Peninsula, Koo Wee Rup, Bass Coast and Southern Gippsland.

We have more than 15 main sites, including hospitals, centres and clinics that provide comprehensive care from welcoming newborns to supporting older people and a full range of services in between.

Bayside Health was formed following the merger of Alfred Health, Bass Coast Health, Gippsland Southern Health Service, Kooweerup Regional Health Service and Peninsula Health on 1 January 2026.

The Team & Role

The Finance Analyst position sits within the Finance and Commercial Directorate, which provides organisation wide leadership in financial stewardship, performance management, compliance and strategic resource planning. The role operates specifically within the Performance Analysis Reporting Unit (PARU), a specialist analytics and reporting function responsible for delivering high quality financial analysis, performance insights and data-driven reporting frameworks that support executive and operational decision-making. PARU plays a key role in strengthening organisational transparency, accountability and performance monitoring by integrating financial, activity and operational data into meaningful intelligence to guide both strategic priorities and day-to-day decision-making.

Position Summary

The Finance Analyst is a senior analytical role, operating with a high degree of professional autonomy and judgement to support financial sustainability and performance. The role provides advanced financial modelling, performance analysis, budgeting insight and activity-based evaluation to support strategic and operational decision-making across the Peninsula Care Group.

Working within PARU, the role strengthens organisational financial sustainability through high-level analytical expertise, enhanced reporting frameworks and robust planning tools. The position collaborates closely with Finance Business Partners, Activity Based Funding and Activity Based Costing team, Financial Reporting and operational leaders, to deliver accurate, timely and actionable financial insights that support organisational performance, accountability and continuous improvement.

Please click here to see the position description.






Skills & Experience Required

Apply advanced professional expertise to interpret complex financial, operational and activity-based data, delivering insights that inform high-level planning and resource allocation.
Lead development and enhancement of forecasting, costing, workforce and activity-based models using advanced Excel, Power Query, Power BI and enterprise finance systems.
Provide senior analytical support for annual budgets, forecasting cycles, performance targets and long-term financial sustainability initiatives.
Prepare high-quality financial and activity performance reports that support executive, board and directorate decision-making.
Undertake complex analysis to support activity-based funding allocation, efficiency reviews, value-for-money assessments and service evaluation.
Consolidate and synthesise insights from Finance Business Partners and system data sources to produce comprehensive performance narratives.
Provide expert advice on financial governance, system optimisation, data quality improvement and reporting methodologies within PARU.
Support Finance Business Partners and Senior Management on business cases, assessment of proposed investments, service changes, and business improvement projects through high-level analytical modelling.
Strengthening business intelligence and data capability across the Peninsula Care Group by improving data structures, reports, dashboards, templates and analytical workflows.
Key Capabilities

Analytical and Technical Expertise

Demonstrated ability to interpret complex, multi-source data sets and translate them into strategic insights.
Expert proficiency in:
Advanced Excel (complex formulas, Power Query, data modelling, automation).
Power BI (data modelling, DAX, dashboard development).
Power Query / ETL processes
Enterprise finance and reporting systems

Strategic and Conceptual Thinking

Ability to integrate financial, operational and activity-based insights into strategic recommendations that influence executive decision-making.

Professional Judgement and Autonomy

Demonstrated capacity to exercise independent judgment, resolve complex analytical issues and operate with minimal direction, consistent with the classification level for this role.

Influential Communication

Ability to present complex financial information clearly and persuasively to executives, clinical leaders, managers and governance committees.

Continuous Improvement and Systems Thinking

Commitment to improving analytic maturity, data governance, reporting frameworks and financial planning processes across the organisation.

Our client is a large, commercially focused utilities company with a strong reputation in the Canberra market.
 
They are an employer of choice in the Canberra region due to their high-performance culture, flexible working arrangements and employee benefits.
 
The Opportunity

There is an opportunity for a Senior Business Planning Officer to join the team, commencing before 1 May 2026 on a 6-month labour hire contract with a potential 6-month extension.
 
The organisation is in its first year of delivering its 10‑year business strategy and is seeking an experienced Business Planning Officer to support the development of the 2026–27 business plans and associated strategy implementation activities.
 
This is a unique opportunity to play a key role in translating strategy into practical, business-ready plans within a collaborative and fast-paced environment. There is flexibility to work from home up to 3 days per week, however you must be able to attend a series of face-to-face workshops scheduled throughout May 2026.
 
The Role

Your key responsibilities will include:

Support the planning, facilitation and documentation of organisation-wide business planning workshops
Provide high-quality scribing and translate workshop outcomes into clear, structured documentation
Develop practical planning and implementation materials aligned to the 10-year strategy
Contribute to broader strategy implementation initiatives, including:
Developing tools and templates to support business planning across the organisation
Reviewing and improving SharePoint content for usability and accessibility
Mapping strategy pathways, sequencing and dependencies
Deliver outputs within tight timeframes, particularly during peak planning periods
The Ideal Person

You will have at least 3 years’ experience in business planning, strategy, corporate support or similar roles within medium to large organisations.
 
You will also bring:

Experience preparing for and supporting the delivery of workshops with diverse stakeholder groups and senior executives
High level secretariat and scribing experience, with the ability to translate workshop discussions into succinct and accurate business plans
The ability to manage competing priorities and deliver high-quality work under tight deadlines
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with experience preparing clear, professional documentation and presentations, including for senior and executive audiences
Tertiary qualifications in business, public policy, or a related discipline
To be eligible for this role, you must have full working rights in Australia and be willing to undergo a police check.
 
Salary / Rate
$85/hr - $90/hr + 12% Super (includes casual loading)
Initial 6-month labour hire contract, with potential 6-month extension
Office located in Mitchell, Canberra ACT
Hybrid working arrangement, with at least 2 days per week in the office

Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please contact Liz Marsden on 02 6108 4878 quoting ref no. 21469.

Handson experience leading platform migrations (e.g. Informatica to Atacama) with minimal business disruption

Ad Text:
Company description:



Queensland Rail's purpose is to provide a customer-focused, safe, reliable, on-time, value for money rail service that benefits the community, supports industry and is integrated with the public transport system. The organisation's vision is connecting communities through a modern, world-class rail service.



Job description:



You'll connect Queensland. We'll connect you to an amazing career.



Queensland Rail stands on the foundation of true connection, encompassing the relationships we foster within our teams and the communities we serve across the state. For over 150 years, we've been instrumental in linking people and places, and now we invite you to contribute to our proud legacy and shape a brighter, sustainable future.

Your Opportunity

Queensland Rail is seeking a Senior Network Capability Analyst to provide authoritative modelling and simulation expertise supporting the performance, resilience and future growth of the South East Queensland rail network.



In this role, you will lead complex network capability analysis across current and future operating states, providing evidence‑based advice that directly informs service planning, infrastructure investment and network change decisions. You will work closely with planning, operations, infrastructure and asset teams to translate system complexity into clear, defensible insights.



This is a technically senior role suited to an experienced rail modeller who can operate with autonomy, influence outcomes, and maintain high analytical standards in a complex environment.


Key Responsibilities:

Lead the development and maintenance of end‑to‑end rail network simulation models representing current and future network states
Deliver absolute capability and capacity assessments integrating infrastructure, signalling, rollingstock and timetable assumptions
Build, validate and stress‑test concept timetables, including major event and future service scenarios
Analyse and articulate network constraints related to signalling layouts, junctions, turnouts, stabling access and run times
Apply robust modelling methodologies to identify true performance limits rather than relying on static assumptions
Establish and uphold modelling data standards, assumptions and governance to ensure consistency and auditability
Translate complex modelling outputs into clear operational and strategic advice for senior stakeholders
Support long‑term planning and investment decisions through multi‑horizon modelling and scenario analysis
Act as a trusted technical authority, advising stakeholders on the appropriate use and limitations of modelling outputs





About you:

Proven experience in rail network modelling, simulation or capacity analysis within a complex operational environment
Strong analytical capability with experience managing, validating and interpreting large, interdependent datasets
Sound understanding of rail operations, signalling principles, timetabling and infrastructure interactions
Experience using rail simulation tools such as OpenTrack, TRENO Suite, or equivalent (highly desirable)
Ability to exercise engineering judgement, challenge assumptions and improve analytical quality
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence through clear, evidence‑based advice
Ability to work autonomously while collaborating effectively across multi‑disciplinary teams

For further details please view the Position Description



Benefits that Count


All that Queensland Rail achieves each day relies on the hard work and talent of our teams. That's why we offer a range of attractive benefits designed to help you bring your best, including:



Starting base salary $134,736 + 12.75% Superannuation
Hybrid work - with 50% work from home.
Enjoy free travel to work and discounted rail travel
QRI Lifestyle offers members access to over 2,000 exclusive discounts and services
Prioritise your physical, emotional and financial wellbeing with our range of free services
You and your family can get fit and have fun with access to over 380 gyms and pools with our Fitness Passport
Shape your rewarding career with clear pathways, innovative projects and opportunities for growth and leadership




Sounds great! What's next?

Please apply for the Senior Network Capability Analyst position via our website by clicking onto the "Apply" button by 11:59pm (GMT+10:00 Brisbane) 19th April 2026.




Supporting a Diverse Workforce

At Queensland Rail we are 1TEAM and our diverse workforce enables us to connect communities every day. We are committed to building inclusive and respectful workplaces for all our people, strengthened by our diversity and encourage applications from First Nations Peoples, all genders, those from culturally diverse backgrounds, LGBTIQ+ community and people with disability, of all ages and backgrounds.



We strive to provide a barrier free and equitable recruitment experience.



If you do require an adjustment during the recruitment process, please contact Theresa via the phone number or email below.



Applicants for roles within Queensland Rail will be subject to Alcohol and Other Drugs pre-employment testing if progressed through the recruitment processes. The recruitment and selection process for this position might require shortlisted applicants to undertake psychometric assessment, pre-employment health assessment and background checks that might also include criminal history and credit checks. For roles that have pre-employment qualifications, QR will also be required to do formal qualification verification processes to ensure qualifications align to QR's requirements.



To learn more about this Queensland Rail career opportunity please contact Theresa with your specific enquiry via Theresa.dickson@qr.com.au Please do not email your application. We can only accept applications received via our website.

Job Title: Senior Risk Analyst - Governance and Accountability (Finance)
Ad Text:
At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined, which is why we support the diverse needs of everyone and provide flexibility to help you thrive.

As one of Australia's largest superannuation funds, we're focused on delivering strong outcomes for our members. A strong risk culture underpins everything we do, and our Governance & Enablement team plays a critical role in supporting sustainable growth, resilience and regulatory confidence.


Your New Role

In this 9-month contract, you'll partner with business units across the Finance Domain to enable effective management of their business risk frameworks in line with the Fund's overall Risk Management Framework.

You'll act as a trusted advisor, embedding risk practices into day‑to‑day operations, supporting informed decision‑making and ensuring business risks, controls and obligations are clearly understood, monitored and reported.

Key duties include but are not limited to;

Partnering with designated business units to provide end‑to‑end business risk and control support.

Monitoring and reporting on the risk and control environment, keeping management informed of key issues and trends.

Supporting the identification, assessment and documentation of risks and controls within the GRC system, including incidents, control self‑assessments and action tracking.

Facilitating root cause analysis of incidents and issues, identifying control weaknesses and improvement opportunities.

Conducting control design and effectiveness testing as part of the control review program and following up on remediation actions.

Supporting the implementation of the Fund's Risk Management Framework, including risk appetite assessments and key risk indicators.

Developing clear risk reporting and dashboards for senior management, governance forums and committees.

Engaging with internal and external auditors and supporting the timely remediation of audit findings.


What You'll Need

Relevant tertiary qualifications in commerce, finance or a related discipline, with industry qualifications or accreditations considered beneficial.

Experience in operational risk, internal or external audit, or risk and control roles within a large financial services organisation.

Strong understanding of risk management frameworks and first, second or third line risk practices.

Exposure to risk and control identification, testing methodologies and incident management.

Highly developed analytical skills with strong attention to detail.

Clear written and verbal communication skills, with confidence engaging and influencing senior stakeholders.

A collaborative, proactive approach and the ability to manage competing priorities in a changing environment.

Experience with Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) systems will be highly regarded.


Life at AustralianSuper

AustralianSuper is committed to supporting your development through ongoing learning, coaching and career opportunities across our growing organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and a blended working environment, and we're open to discussing flexibility that works for you.

We cultivate a workplace built on safety, respect and inclusiveness, where diversity is valued and everyone feels they belong.


What's Next

Apply now if you share our values of Energy, Integrity, Generosity of Spirit and Excellent Outcomes, and you're excited by the opportunity to make a meaningful impact for millions of members.

AustralianSuper uses AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. This involves processing your resume, cover letter and voice recording in Australia and the USA to help assess your suitability for the role. When invited to an AI phone screening, you may opt for a traditional phone screening instead. For information on how we handle your personal data, please refer to our Candidate Privacy Policy:

https://www.australiansuper.com/careers/candidate-privacy-notice

AustralianSuper is committed to providing an inclusive recruitment experience. If you require reasonable adjustments at any stage of the process, please let our Talent Acquisition team know.

Agencies please note: this vacancy is being managed directly by AustralianSuper's Talent Acquisition team. We will contact our preferred agency partners should we require additional support. Thank you.

This position is responsible for driving end‑to‑end cashflow reporting, including oversight of cashflow forecasting, liquidity analysis, and regulatory cashflow requirements across the AGL Group.
A core focus of the role is delivering robust group reporting and consolidation, ensuring accurate, timely, and insightful management reporting that supports senior finance leaders and the Board.

Working closely with senior finance stakeholders, this role will deliver on value‑adding analysis that strengthens AGL's financial governance and business decisions, as well as continuous improvement initiatives to enhance efficiency, accuracy and reporting quality.

What You'll Do

Cash Flow

Planning, Analysis and Reporting

Assist in analysing AGL Energy progress towards a range of market targets and initiatives.


Finance Initiatives and Ad-hoc Analysis

​What you'll bring



CPA/CA qualified + demonstrated experience in a group reporting role (ideally within or for an ASX-listed organisation)

Ad Text:
Deliver high-impact data science and AI solutions from concept to production

Lead research applying data science, AI and machine learning to central banking challenges

Uplift data science and AI capability through technical leadership and mentoring

Are you an experienced data scientist who enjoys balancing hands-on delivery with applied research and technical leadership? Join the Reserve Bank of Australia as a Principal Data Scientist in Economic Research, where you will apply data science, AI and machine learning to central banking challenges, contribute to policy relevant research and build data science capability across the Bank.

About the team

The Data Science Hub, part of the Economic Research Department, is the hub of a vibrant and growing data science community at the Bank. We combine policy‑relevant research with practical delivery, translating advanced data science into impactful analytical tools and insights that support the Bank's understanding of the economy and monetary policy.

About the role

As a Principal Data Scientist at the Reserve Bank of Australia, you will provide technical leadership and drive the application of data science and AI to central banking questions spanning monetary policy, financial stability and operational decision making. Balancing applied research with practical delivery, you will collaborate with economists and other stakeholders to translate complex analytical work into meaningful insights, while contributing to the growth of the Bank's data science capability and setting a high technical bar for the team.

What You'll Do


Lead end-end delivery of high impact data science projects, from scoping and prioritisation through to deployment.

Lead and contribute to rigorous research applying data science, AI and non-traditional data to central banking challenges.

Uplift data science capability across the Bank by championing advanced methodologies, providing mentoring and embedding best practices across teams.

Provide deep technical expertise, working with IT and other stakeholders to shape platforms, infrastructure and governance that supports current and future data science needs.

Translate complex analytics into actionable insights for senior policymakers and non-technical audiences.

About you


Experience leading complex data science projects from concept through to delivery.

Demonstrated research capability in data science, including experience producing high quality written work (e.g publications, working papers or applied research), with the ability to apply research thinking to practical problems.

Deep technical expertise in modern data science techniques (e.g. machine learning, NLP, statistical modelling) and associated tools and platforms

Experience providing technical leadership, mentoring and helping others build strong data science skills.

Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to collaborate across disciplines and backgrounds to build alignment on objectives and deliver good outcomes.

Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical and senior audiences.

Familiarity with macroeconomics, financial markets, financial stability, and monetary policy frameworks is highly regarded.

A research‑focused postgraduate qualification (e.g. PhD or Master's by Research), or equivalent research experience, is preferred.

Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

Ad Text:
About us

At CareSuper, we believe we’re Super Together. As a profit-to-member super fund with over 600,000 members and $60 billion in assets, we’re passionate about helping our members retire with confidence. We’re growing fast and looking for our next superhero to join us on this exciting journey.

About the role

As our new Data Engineering Specialist, you’ll be part of our Technology Delivery team. Reporting to the Manager – Enterprise Systems, you will be responsible for the design, development and optimisation of our enterprise data platform built on Azure Databricks. You will deliver scalable, cloud-native data engineering solutions in line with our Data Strategy and roadmap, enabling analytics, reporting and advanced insights across the organisation.

Please note, we are open to candidates located anywhere on the East Coast of Australia (Melbourne, Hobart, Brisbane, Sydney) for this position.

A day in the life may look like;

Providing technical and strategic advice to senior management, staff and stakeholders on Care Super’s cloud data platform, data pipelines and analytics enablement.
Designing, building and optimising data ingestion, transformation and orchestration pipelines using Azure Databricks and associated Azure services.
Troubleshooting and performance-tuning data engineering workloads, notebooks, jobs and supporting services to ensure reliability and scalability.
Producing and reviewing technical documentation, solution designs and data models, and analysing business data sources to support analytical and operational use cases.
Providing guidance and support in the use and development of modern analytics and data engineering tools, enabling self-service analytics where appropriate.
Completing escalated Level 3 data platform requests and supporting the Insights and Analytics team to resolve complex data issues and queries.
What we’re looking for

Our ideal candidate will bring strong experience designing and delivering cloud-based data engineering solutions, with a focus on Azure and Databricks. You’ll also bring the following skills:

Strong SQL and data modelling skills across relational and analytical data stores
Data ingestion, transformation and orchestration using Azure Databricks (Spark, SQL, Python/Scala)
Experience with Delta Lake, medallion architecture and modern data platform design patterns
Integration with Azure services such as Azure Data Lake Storage, Azure Data Factory, Azure Synapse and Power BI
Knowledge of Agile development methodologies and best practices
CI/CD practices for data platforms (e.g. Azure DevOps, Git-based workflows)
Experience supporting analytics, dashboards and KPIs using Databricks or Power BI
Strong documentation, stakeholder engagement and problem-solving skills

Mansell Taylor Consulting is proud to partner with Transmax to appoint a Head of Quality & Delivery Excellence, responsible for improving engineering quality, delivery flow, and release governance across the organisation.

This is a senior leadership role focused on enabling consistent, high-quality, and predictable software delivery.

The opportunity

You will lead Quality Engineering and Agile delivery capability, embedding modern testing practices, delivery frameworks, and supporting delivery excellence and cross-cutting initiatives across all our product, platform, and operational teams.

This role plays a key part in strengthening engineering performance, improving delivery predictability, and supporting safe, scalable releases, including being a critical part of our AI-enabled process across our SDLC.

What you’ll be doing

Lead Quality Engineering and Scrum Master capability across teams
Define and implement testing strategies and automation frameworks
Improve delivery flow using Agile practices and performance metrics
Establish and govern release and change management processes
Embed modern delivery practices (CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Partner with SRE and Architecture on resilience and NFR alignment
Drive delivery metrics, reporting, and continuous improvement
Lead release governance and approval processes
Enable SDLC standards and engineering capability uplift

About you

Proven leadership in quality engineering or software delivery environments
Strong background in Agile, testing automation, and SDLC practices, understanding where the theory meets pragmatism to deliver measurable outcomes across the way people work in a software business
Experience implementing modern testing frameworks (unit, integration, E2E, performance)
Strong understanding of release governance and delivery risk
Experience in regulated or mission-critical environments
Strong stakeholder engagement across technical and business teams
Leadership experience across multidisciplinary teams
Tertiary qualification in IT, Software Engineering, or equivalent experience

Why apply

Lead delivery quality and engineering capability at scale
Shape modern software delivery practices
High-impact role across product and platform teams
Work closely with senior technology leadership

Apply

Apply now or contact Jelissa Taylor on 0415 430 303. Applications close Friday 17 April.

Category: Commercial Sales, Leasing & Property Mgmt (Real Estate & Property)
Ad Text:
Abacus is seeking an experienced Senior Portfolio Manager to play a critical role in maximising returns from a national commercial property portfolio. Working closely with the General Manager – Commercial Portfolio, this role is responsible for driving long‑term asset value, optimising income and expenditure, strengthening customer relationships, and supporting the broader Commercial team through strategic leadership and expertise.




About Abacus

Abacus Group is a strong asset-backed, annuity style business model where capital is directed towards assets that provide potential for enhanced growth and ultimately create value. Our people, market insight and repositioning capability together with strategic partnering are the key enablers of our strategy.




About the role

This is a senior asset management role with a strong focus on strategy, value creation, customer centricity and consistent service delivery across a diverse commercial portfolio.

Your key responsibilities will include:

Develop and deliver portfolio and asset management strategies to maximise income, value and investment returns.

Lead medium‑ to long‑term asset planning, including capital deployment, income optimisation, expense management and risk mitigation.

Undertake strategic asset and market analysis to ensure assets are competitively positioned.

Drive portfolio performance through leasing strategy, including appointment and management of external leasing agents and direct lease negotiations.

Build strong, trusted relationships with customers, investors and key external stakeholders.

Partner with Property and Facility Managers to ensure excellence in asset operations and tenant experience.

Manage income, operating and capital budgets in collaboration with Finance and internal stakeholders.

Contribute to high‑quality Board and internal reporting and strategic business planning.

Mentor and coach Portfolio Managers and Analysts, providing leadership across strategic asset management practices.

About you

You are a commercially minded property professional with a strong strategic lens and a passion for value creation. You bring credibility, gravitas and a proactive, ownership‑driven approach to your work.

To be successful in this role you will ideally have:

A minimum of 7 years’ experience in commercial portfolio or asset management.

Proven experience delivering long‑term asset strategies and strong financial outcomes.

Tertiary qualifications in Property or a related discipline.

Strong financial acumen, analytical capability and commercial judgement.

Excellent negotiation, deal‑making and relationship management skills.

A customer‑centric mindset with a strong focus on service quality and tenant experience.

High‑level written and verbal communication skills, with experience preparing Board‑level reporting.

The ability to work autonomously while collaborating effectively across a high‑performing team.

You’ll be part of a forward‑thinking commercial property business that values entrepreneurship, accountability and teamwork. This role offers the opportunity to influence portfolio strategy at a senior level, work with high‑quality assets and stakeholders, and make a meaningful impact on long‑term investment performance. In return, you will be rewarded with a supportive and engaging work environment with a focus on your development in the property sector.

Successful candidates will also need to complete Abacus vetting processes including reference and police checks. 

To apply please click the “Apply” button below.

Deputy is a global SaaS workforce management company with hubs in Sydney, Melbourne, San Francisco and London, plus team members working remotely across the United States. Our platform serves over 1.5 million workers and 375,000 workplaces across 100+ countries. We are backed by top global investors and recently achieved Unicorn status. 

At Deputy, we’re improving the world of work, one shift at a time, for 80% of the world’s workforce: hourly workers. These are the dedicated employees who keep our world running – from baristas to nurses, cleaners to delivery drivers, florists to factory workers. Despite their vital role in society, most workplace technology has focused on those workers who sit behind a desk, but at Deputy, we transform the frontline. When businesses use Deputy, their workplaces thrive – the business is more profitable, compliant, and productive, while the workers are more engaged and happier at work.

We're becoming an AI-native company, a commitment that means you'll be empowered (and expected) to use AI tools and thinking in your day-to-day work. You'll have the training, support, and freedom to use AI responsibly and creatively to spark ideas, solve problems faster, and unlock new ways of working.

If you’re passionate about creating solutions that put people first and helping businesses and their teams thrive, join us at Deputy and make an impact where it matters most!

We're looking for an experienced Director, Revenue Operations, Systems & Enablement to help support and scale the Commercial organization (Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, Support, Partnerships). The candidate must have a strong foundation in business operations, technical systems architecture, and team enablement. You will not only drive operational strategy and board-level reporting but also own the technical roadmap for our business systems (including billing) and ensure our GTM teams are trained and equipped to sell effectively.

This is a great opportunity to create scalable processes, build a robust technical foundation, and foster a culture of continuous learning in a fast-growing company. The role will report to the Commercial Chief of Staff.

Responsibilities:

Global Revenue Operations: Develop and execute Global Revenue Operations by collaborating with Sales, CS, and Marketing Leadership to drive key strategic initiatives.

Enablement Strategy: Own the end-to-end enablement strategy, including onboarding, continuous learning, product training, and sales process methodologies for all GTM teams.

Systems Architecture & Billing: Lead the strategic design and technical architecture of the business systems stack, serving as the owner for billing technology (Zuora) and its integration with CRM (Salesforce) and ERP systems.

Process Optimization: Optimize sales and billing processes across Quote-to-Cash, Deal Desk, Forecasting, Commissions, and GTM Strategy to ensure seamless execution.

Board & Executive Reporting: Assist CCO in design, creation, and execution of board communications/decks and weekly/quarterly business reviews.

Quality & Consistency: Proactively monitor and maintain high levels of data quality, accuracy, and process consistency across the Revenue organization.

Metrics & Dashboards: Develop key performance metrics, dashboards, and reports that help the GTM organization focus on performance drivers and results.

Cross-Functional Planning: Partner with Finance to drive annual and long-term planning, budgeting, business KPIs, and process alignment.

Key Areas of Focus & Collaboration:

STRATEGY & ENABLEMENT:

GTM Enablement: Design and deliver comprehensive training programs for Sales, CS, and Partnerships, covering product launches, sales methodology, and systems adoption.

Onboarding: Build and manage a best-in-class onboarding program to reduce ramp time for new commercial hires.

Content & Playbooks: Partner with Product Marketing to ensure GTM teams are equipped with up-to-date content, playbooks, and competitive intelligence.

Strategic Planning: Collaborate with Finance and CCO on org structure, sales strategy, and long-term planning cycles.

BUSINESS SYSTEMS & ARCHITECTURE:

Billing Infrastructure (Zuora): Own the technical implementation and management of Zuora, ensuring accurate billing, subscription management, and revenue recognition compliance.

Technical Architecture: Design and maintain a scalable GTM systems architecture, ensuring seamless data flow between Salesforce, Zuora, Marketing Automation (Marketo, Customer.io), and other tools.

Integrations: Oversee system integrations and API workflows to support a low-friction, automated Quote-to-Cash process.

Tool Management: Manage vendor selection, procurement, and optimization for the entire GTM tech stack.

PROCESS & OPERATIONS:

Quote-to-Cash: Streamline the end-to-end deal lifecycle, from CPQ to billing and revenue recognition.

Sales Process: Define and enforce policies for Deal Desk, forecasting, and pipeline management.

Operational Cadence: Drive the WW Sales and Marketing cadence (e.g., All Hands, WBRs, QBRs, SKO).

PLANNING & METRICS:

Analytics: drive deep analysis of sales capacity, territory segmentation, and funnel performance.

Compensation: Manage compensation strategy, design, compliance, and production.

You Have:

10+ years of relevant experience in a software business, with a focus on Revenue Operations, Systems, or Enablement.

Deep technical expertise in Business Systems, specifically Zuora (Billing) and Salesforce (SFDC). Experience with architecture and integration (middleware, APIs) is highly preferred.

Proven experience building or managing GTM Enablement functions, including creating curriculum, training sales teams, and managing content.

Excellent quantitative analytical skills and a keen business sense to drive decision-making.

Ability to communicate complex technical and operational concepts to executive leadership.

Experience managing cross-functional projects that touch Finance, Product, and Sales.

Ability to coach, motivate, and recruit a high-performing operations and systems team.

You share our values and work in accordance with those values.

Job Title: Strategy and Operations Lead, Regulation, AUNZ (Fixed-Term Contract)
Ad Text:
This is a fixed-term contract for a duration of 12 months.

At Google, we have a vision of empowerment and equitable opportunity for all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and commit to building reconciliation through Google’s technology, platforms and people and we welcome Indigenous applicants. Please see our Reconciliation Action Plan for more information.

Minimum qualifications:

Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
6 years of experience with go-to-market strategy, sales operations, strategy and operations, management consulting, finance, or program management.
Preferred qualifications:

Experience in working with strategy in ambiguous settings, with strategy implementation.
Knowledge of the Australian regulatory and policy environment.
Excellent executive presence and collaboration skills.
Excellent business acumen and judgement.
Excellent problem-solving skills with the ability to present problem statements, perform data analysis and investigations, and provide recommendations that drive commercial value.
About the job
The Go-to-Market Operations (GTM) team ensures Google's business runs smoothly by aligning priorities with execution.


In this role, you will act as a key business partner to the Country Manager, driving sustainable growth through process improvements and data-motivated insights. You will problem solving and qualified strategist and project manager, capable of providing clarity and building relationships across local and global cross-functional teams to deliver business outcomes. You will develop strategies that support leadership decision making, cross-functional alignment of goals and expectations, and help teams to execute plans. You will develop and drive business strategy in collaboration with the executive business partners, acting as a trusted advisor, prioritiser and direction setter. You will help the organization navigate internal complexities, external factors, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to set strategy, solve problems, and design, manage, and deliver projects. You will help to set the focus, pace and direction of the business across teams to deliver on business opportunities by advocating, testing, and enabling the cross-functional teams. You will focus on partnering with the cross-functional Australia and New Zealand (AUNZ) leadership, particularly around regulatory topics.


Responsibilities

Develop cross-functional plans, and deliver insights and recommendations that shape the AUNZ operating strategy. Define and solve problems and opportunities that impact the corporate reputation.
Partner with the Google AUNZ leadership team and global stakeholders to provide guidance and coordination on regulatory topics.
Establish and maintain business cadences and internal stakeholder relationships to deliver together on priorities and best practices.
Develop external intelligence and insights in the market, to enable cross-functional decision-making. Collaborate with executive stakeholders through strategy development and program management.
Lead planning and execution for the AUNZ business, collaborating with the cross-functional leadership team to identify and prioritize key focus areas. Enhance initiatives by incorporating program management best practices, data-motivated insights, and executive communication strategies.
Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

Are you a proactive Change Analyst looking to make a tangible impact within a complex, large-scale operational environment? This 6-month initial contract offers a unique opportunity to lead critical change activities and drive organisational readiness for a major program of work. You will join a team focused on delivering sustainable, efficient solutions while navigating a sophisticated, industrially sensitive landscape. With a strong likelihood of extension, this role is perfect for a specialist who thrives on building stakeholder buy-in and translating high-level requirements into successful business outcomes.

What You Will Do:
Deliver end-to-end change activities including assessment, analysis, and documentation to ensure the successful implementation of program objectives.
Conduct comprehensive change impact assessments using established methodologies such as PROSCI/ADKAR to support management activities.
Identify, analyse, and prepare risk mitigation tactics while proactively identifying and managing anticipated resistance to change.
Collaborate with HR, IR, LD, Communications, and other operational business groups to deliver an integrated people change service.
Build and maintain robust relationships with internal and external stakeholders to enhance project delivery and resolve engagement issues.
What You Need to Apply for This Role:
Relevant tertiary qualification or equivalent professional experience in change management.
Proven experience managing change within a highly industrialised or complex operational landscape.
Demonstrated ability to drive project milestones and deadlines while balancing the diverse needs of multiple stakeholder groups.
Strong proficiency in change management methodologies and the ability to coach or mentor team members in these practices.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly and write fluently for diverse audiences.
Randstad contractor benefits:
Access to a Udemy license for ongoing learning and continuous professional development
Access to exclusive discounts across various platforms
Discounts on private health funds
3 payruns a week

Interested? Apply Now!

If you are a Change Analyst with experience in a highly industrialised landscape, we want to hear from you. Click "Apply Now".

You must be a Sydney based candidate with full working rights to work in Australia to be considered.

Senior Data Quality Analyst | Banking | Sydney CBD

We are seeking a Senior Data Quality Analyst to join a high-profile, large-scale data migration program for a leading bank. This is a hands-on execution role focusing on customer data, product arrangements, and complex validation within a Product Model framework.

Location: Sydney CBD (Hybrid - 3 days in office) | Permanent Role

The Role

Working closely with the Data Quality Delivery Lead, you will ensure the integrity of data moving through a complex migration lifecycle. You'll be the bridge between Business, Tech, and Operations to ensure data is "fit for purpose."

Data Validation: Perform individual record validation and volumetric analysis on customer and banking product data.

Technical Execution: Drive data profiling, defect triage, and remediation tracking.

Coordination: Manage technology release activities and ensure evidence is produced for migration milestones.

Governance: Develop DQ rules, validation evidence, and reporting packs for executive stakeholders.

Technical Toolkit

SQL & Python: Strong capability for data manipulation and analysis.

Visualization: Power BI for DQ reporting and dashboards.

Cloud: Experience working within Azure Cloud environments.

Tooling: Informatica Data Quality (IDQ) is highly regarded (nice to have).

Your Background

Banking Experience: Deep understanding of banking products, customer data, and regulated environments is a significant plus.

Migration Expertise: Proven track record in large-scale data migration or transformation programs.

Data Modeling: Familiarity with Common Product Models and complex product arrangements.

Delivery Focus: Comfortable navigating ambiguity and working in a fast-paced, structured delivery environment.
